coaching.coaching: Plan how verification commands for arch.md deletion will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification commands for arch.md deletion

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the arch.md row deletion in AC3, e.g. `grep -c 'lint_refs' docs/architecture.md` asserting 0 matches (or a targeted `grep -v` confirming the offenders-table row is absent).
evidence: AC4 proves allowlist/ceilings changes: 'python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q'
AC6 proves lint passes: 'make lint prints All checks passed!'
No command specifically targets docs/architecture.md content to confirm row absence
ABSENCE: no grep or diff command anchored to the arch.md offenders table row for lint_refs.py
findings.finding: AC3 states that the `docs/architecture.md` remedy row must be deleted, but no proving command confirms this deletion. `make lint` (AC6) is a catch-all that may or may not validate arch.md content, and AC4 (module-size contract test) only verifies the allowlist/ceilings files. An agent could skip the arch.md edit and still pass all stated commands.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC3: 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md'
scenarios: Agent deletes lint_refs.py from allowlist/ceilings (AC4 passes), skips the arch.md row deletion, and all ACs still pass — leaving a stale superseded row in architecture docs.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: verify with `grep -c 'lint_refs.py' docs/architecture.md` returning 0, or `! grep -q 'lint_refs.py' docs/architecture.md`.

evidence: verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_expr.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether AC3 should also require adding a resolution note or updated row to arch.md documenting that lint_refs.py was brought under cap via lint_expr.py extraction, or explicitly state that deletion alone is the correct convention for resolved entries.
evidence: AC3: 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md'
Deviation section: 'This ticket deletes that stale/superseded arch.md row'
No AC or plan prose requires adding an updated row or note recording the resolution of the lint_refs.py oversize issue
findings.finding: AC3 verifies deletion of the stale arch.md remedy row but does not require updating arch.md to document the actual resolution — that lint_expr.py was created and lint_refs.py was brought under cap. After the ticket, arch.md's offenders table will have a gap where the row was, with no record of what was done.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC3; 'Deviation from docs/architecture.md' section
scenarios: A future reviewer of arch.md sees an incomplete offenders table with no record of the lint_refs.py resolution, making the table's history opaque.
If arch.md tracks active oversized modules only (not resolutions), the deletion is sufficient — but the plan never clarifies which convention applies.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief entry for `lint_expr.py` in `docs/architecture.md` (e.g., in the module inventory or dependency graph) to document the new expression-validation leaf, not just delete the old remedy row.
evidence: Plan states 'This ticket deletes that stale/superseded arch.md row' but is silent about adding lint_expr.py anywhere in architecture.md
The plan introduces a new stdlib-leaf module that is architecturally significant (it establishes a new expression-validation boundary), yet the only arch.md change described is a deletion
findings.finding: The plan deletes the arch.md remedy/offenders-table row for lint_refs.py but does not mention adding lint_expr.py to any module inventory, dependency graph, or descriptive listing in docs/architecture.md. If architecture.md tracks the workflow-layer module set (beyond the offenders table), it will be stale with respect to the new leaf.
findings.location: Deviation from docs/architecture.md section; AC line 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md'
scenarios: A developer consulting architecture.md to understand the workflow-layer module graph finds no entry for lint_expr.py, leading to confusion about where expression validation lives.
A future module-size or dependency audit misses lint_expr.py because it is not listed in the architecture doc.
findings.suggested_fix: In the architecture.md update, after removing the lint_refs.py remedy row, add one line to the workflow-layer module table/graph noting that `lint_expr.py` is a pure-stdlib leaf owning the single-expression validator, with an inbound edge from lint_refs.py.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
